channel.title: Python on DailyPyTips
channel.description: Recent content in Python on DailyPyTips

item.title: Day 3: Your Data Structures Are Not What You Think (Lists, Tuples, Sets Internals)
item.description:
You think a list is just “a collection of items.”
It’s not.

It’s a memory-managed data structure with trade-offs.

Today, you stop using data structures blindly.

Today’s Goal

By the end of today, you will:

  • Understand how lists, tuples, and sets work internally
  • Learn their time and space complexity
  • Choose the right structure based on behavior and cost

The Illusion

You write:

data = [1, 2, 3]

Reality:

This is a dynamic array of references

item.title: Day 1: Python Is Not Running Your Code (Execution Model Deep Dive)
item.description:
You write Python.
But Python is not executing your code the way you think.

Today, you stop treating Python as magic.

Today’s Goal

By the end of today, you will:

  • Understand how Python executes your code
  • Learn what happens between .py → execution
  • Build a mental model of the Python interpreter

The Illusion

You write:

x = 10
y = x + 5
print(y)

You assume:

“Python reads this line by line and runs it”

That assumption is wrong.

item.title: Day 2: Variables Don't Store Values (Python Memory Model)
item.description:
If you think x = 10 means “x stores 10”…
you are already misunderstanding Python.

Today, we fix that.

Today’s Goal

By the end of today, you will:

  • Understand how Python stores data in memory
  • Learn references vs values
  • Understand mutability
  • Avoid hidden bugs

The Illusion

You write:

x = 10
y = x

You think:

“x and y both store 10”

Wrong.

What Actually Happens

Memory: [10] ← object

x ─────┐

 ├──→ [10]

y ─────┘

item.title: Mastering Dicts: The Most Powerful Python Container You are Underusing
item.description: A deep dive into Python dictionaries — how they work, hidden gems like defaultdict and Counter, and how to use them efficiently in real-world scripts.

item.title: From Idea to PyPI — How I Built and Published My Own Python SDK
item.description: Learn step-by-step how to create, test, and publish your own Python SDK to PyPI using the OpenWeather API as an example.

item.title: Python Lists vs Tuples vs Sets — When to Use Each
item.description: Practical differences between Python's list, tuple, and set types — and when each one makes sense in production code.
